title: How AI Endpoints Change the Traditional API Flow
excerpt: As a backend developer, I have built hundreds of endpoints, so the typical endpoint flow is deeply...
content_html:
As a backend developer, I have built hundreds of endpoints, so the typical endpoint flow is deeply ingrained in how I think about web applications. But when I started building AI-powered endpoints, I noticed an interesting shift.

At first, AI endpoints looked like simple proxy endpoints with some configuration for connecting to a model:

API receives request
↓
send prompt to model
↓
receive response
↓
return it to the client

And it worked well until I found out that passing a prompt directly from the client was not a good idea. The endpoint could be misused for a completely different purpose, allowing someone else to consume my AI usage credits.

Then I realized that the input also needed limits. Sending a large context for a specific task costs more and may produce unexpected results.

So when I started looking closer, especially when I needed reliable structured output and predictable application behavior, I quickly realized that it was not that simple. Validation was no longer only guarding execution, it had also become a post-processing step. AI models are probabilistic. Even with the same input, they may return different outputs, omit required information, misunderstand instructions or return something that is technically valid but logically wrong. And because every token has a price, I cannot simply retry the request and hope for a better result.

That was when I started questioning whether AI endpoints should be designed in the same way as conventional Web API endpoints.

Table of Contents

  • Conventional Web API Endpoint Flow
  • AI-powered Web API Endpoint Flow
  • What This Difference Changes
    • Unpredictable Latency
    • Retry Logic
    • Idempotency and Side Effects
    • Testing AI Endpoints
    • The Output Contract
    • Observability and Cost
  • Summary

Conventional Web API Endpoint Flow

A conventional Web API endpoint usually follows a similar flow:

validate request
↓
execute business logic
↓
return representation

The first phase is request validation. We validate the incoming data against property constraints, API contracts, authorization rules and application-specific business rules.

The second phase is execution. The application processes data, performs I/O operations or executes business logic.

The final phase is returning a representation of the result. The code executed by a conventional endpoint is normally deterministic within a known application state. When the same code runs against the same state, we generally get the same result.

And that is basically it.

The general flow of a conventional Web API endpoint is relatively simple, although the individual steps can, of course, be very complex. But AI models do not work in exactly the same way. They do not simply execute a predefined sequence of instructions and always produce the same result.

AI-powered Web API Endpoint Flow

The internal flow of an AI-powered endpoint is usually more complicated:

validate request
↓
prepare prompt, tools and context
↓
generate probabilistic output
↓
validate schema, meaning and safety
↓
retry, repair, reject or fall back
↓
return representation

We still start by validating the incoming request. Standard property constraints and business rules are still important, but AI endpoints may also require additional controls. The application may need to restrict the requested topic, limit input size, apply controls against suspicious instructions, isolate untrusted retrieved content or decide which tools the model is allowed to call.

The next step is preparing the prompt and settings for the model call. The application prepares the instructions, conversation history, retrieved context, tool definitions and generation settings.

Then the model produces an output. But unlike the result of conventional business logic, we cannot automatically assume that the output is correct just because the model call succeeded. A successful HTTP response from the model provider only tells us that the model generated something. It does not tell us whether the result is complete, safe, grounded or even useful.

So validation appears again after generation. For example, we may validate the JSON schema in a similar way to a response from a conventional external service. But even when the schema is correct, the result may still be logically wrong for the business domain. The model may also omit properties that are not technically required by the schema but should be present based on the provided context.

When the output fails these checks, the application must decide what to do next. It may try to repair the response, ask the model to generate it again, use a fallback model, return a controlled error or send the request for human review. But every decision has its own cost. A retry consumes more tokens, while human intervention costs both time and money.

All of this turns the endpoint into an orchestration pipeline instead of a simple proxy around a model call.

What This Difference Changes

With a conventional Web API, the application explicitly defines how the result is created. This means that the execution path is controlled by code and we know what result to expect.

With an AI-powered Web API, the application delegates part of the result creation to a probabilistic system. This means that we no longer fully control how the result is generated. To achieve the expected result, we need to add extra steps to the flow, such as post-processing and output validation.

We can simplify it like this:

  • Conventional Web API: the backend executes the rules.
  • AI-powered Web API: the backend orchestrates and evaluates the result.

But this difference affects much more than validation. It also changes how we think about latency, retries, idempotency, testing, observability, cost and output contracts.

There are definitely more differences, but let’s have a look at the ones I discovered along the way.

Unpredictable Latency

The latency of a conventional endpoint is usually determined by business logic, I/O operations, data processing and similar operations. These operations are not always fast or perfectly predictable, but we can usually measure them separately and optimize the slowest parts by improving the code, optimizing database queries or adding caching.

AI endpoints introduce another level of variability.

Generation time may depend on the selected model, prompt and context size, output length, provider load and other factors. One request may finish after a single model call, while another may require several tool calls, validation attempts or regeneration steps.

So latency is no longer determined only by the operations we explicitly execute. It may also depend on decisions made during model generation.

That makes timeouts, cancellation, streaming, asynchronous processing and latency budgets even more important.

One of the easiest ways to improve the latency of an AI-powered endpoint is to improve the prompt. We can make it more specific, reduce unnecessary context and limit the expected output.

Another example is avoiding the need for the model to return full objects.

For example, we may provide data like this:

{
  "data": [
    {
      "id": 1,
      "name": "john doe",
      "address": "Mordor",
      "category": "Nazgûl"
    },
    {
      "id": 2,
      "name": "joe doe",
      "address": "Gondor",
      "category": "soldier"
    }
  ]
}

Then we can instruct the model to return only the selected IDs instead of repeating the full objects.

The backend can map those IDs back to the original data after generation. This reduces the number of output tokens and may improve both latency and cost.

The main point is that we approach latency optimization differently. With conventional endpoints, we usually optimize code, database access or caching. With AI endpoints, we also need to optimize prompts, context size, model selection and generated output.

Retry Logic

In conventional Web APIs, we often retry I/O operations under specific conditions. For example, we may retry when a dependency is temporarily unavailable, a connection is interrupted or an external service returns a transient status code. These retries are usually based on technical failures.

AI endpoints introduce another type of retry. The request may complete successfully at the transport level, but the generated output may still be unusable. For example, it may miss a required field, break the expected schema, contradict the supplied context or fail a business rule.

Every retry increases latency and consumes additional tokens, which means additional cost. The next attempt may return a different but still incorrect response.

Because of that, AI retries should not be treated like ordinary network retries. They need explicit limits, and we should also consider additional constraints such as the token and monetary budget, the reason for the failure, whether another attempt is likely to help, and whether a fallback model or deterministic alternative is available.

Let’s have a look at this C# retry pipeline, which I used with a local model exposed through Ollama:

private static class RetryPipeline<T>
{
    public static readonly ResiliencePipeline<Result<T>> Instance = new ResiliencePipelineBuilder<Result<T>>()
        .AddRetry(new RetryStrategyOptions<Result<T>>
        {
            MaxRetryAttempts = 2,
            Delay = TimeSpan.FromSeconds(2),
            BackoffType = DelayBackoffType.Exponential,
            UseJitter = true,
            ShouldHandle = new PredicateBuilder<Result<T>>()
                .Handle<HttpRequestException>()
                .Handle<JsonException>()
                .Handle<TaskCanceledException>(ex =>
                   !ex.CancellationToken.IsCancellationRequested)
                .HandleResult(r => r.IsFailed)
        })
        .Build();
}

The pipeline retries when it receives an HttpRequestException, JsonException or TaskCanceledException. It allows a maximum of two retry attempts and uses exponential backoff with an initial delay of two seconds.

So far, this looks like a typical resilience pipeline for a conventional API endpoint.

The important difference is this condition:

.HandleResult(r => r.IsFailed)

It also retries when the operation does not throw an exception but still returns a failed result.

Inside the action executed by this pipeline, I validate the AI output. If the response is technically valid but logically wrong, I return a failed result and let the pipeline try again.

This approach may not be ideal when using a paid model provider because every retry consumes additional tokens and increases the cost. But for a local or free model, a small and strictly limited number of retries may be sufficient.

Sometimes the correct decision is not to retry. It may be better to reject the output, return a controlled error or ask the user for more information.

Idempotency and Side Effects

Idempotency means that executing the same operation multiple times has the same effect as executing it once.

A simple example is a conventional GET endpoint:

GET /api/orders/123

We can call this endpoint several times without changing the order. The returned representation may change if the underlying data changes, but the request itself does not produce additional side effects.

With AI endpoints, idempotency becomes more complicated when the model can call tools.

Sending the same prompt multiple times may produce different outputs or slightly different wording. For read-only endpoints, this may be acceptable because no server state is changed.

But it becomes much more dangerous when the endpoint can perform actions. For example, the model may create an order, send an email or update data through a tool. If output validation later fails and the whole operation is retried, the model may call the same tool again and create a second order.

That is why actions triggered by AI endpoints should use protections such as idempotency keys, persisted operation results and unique constraints. Repeating the same action with the same operation ID should return the original result instead of executing the side effect again.

Testing AI Endpoints

Testing conventional endpoints is relatively straightforward when the application state and dependencies are controlled.

We prepare the data, execute the endpoint and assert the expected result, such as the status code, expected response values, an updated application state or whether an external dependency was called.

With AI endpoints, exact-output assertions are often fragile. The same valid answer may be written in many different ways. A model update may also change the wording while keeping the same meaning. But asserting only the response status or checking that fields are not null is too weak.

Instead of always comparing the exact response, we can verify the required properties of the result. Depending on the use case, we may assert that values are within an allowed range, forbidden content is not present, tool calls respect the allowlist and the response follows the expected business rules.

For example, asserting the complete response with Assert.Equal would be fragile:

var result = await endpoint.GeneratePlanAsync(request);

// Fragile assertion
Assert.Equal("Family Day in Brno", result.Title);
Assert.Equal(
    "Visit the science centre and have lunch nearby.",
    result.Description);
Assert.Equal(1_500, result.TotalCost);

The model may return a different title or description while still producing a completely valid plan.

Instead of checking the exact response, we can assert the properties that matter to the application using methods such as InRange, Contains and DoesNotContain:

var result = await endpoint.GeneratePlanAsync(request);

Assert.NotNull(result);
Assert.NotEmpty(result.Activities);

//Assert
Assert.InRange(result.TotalCost,0,request.Budget);

Assert.All(
    result.Activities,
    activity =>
    {
        Assert.Contains(
            activity.Type,
            request.AllowedActivityTypes);

        Assert.InRange(
            activity.TravelTimeMinutes,
            0,
            request.MaximumTravelMinutes);

        Assert.False(
            string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(activity.Name));
    });

Assert.DoesNotContain(result.Activities, activity => activity.IsClosed);
Assert.All(result.Activities,activity => Assert.NotEmpty(activity.SourceIds));

The title, description or order of activities may change, but the generated plan must still respect the budget, travel-time limits, allowed activity types and supplied source data.

A large part of the endpoint can still be tested deterministically. Schema validation, authorization, tool permissions, parsing, fallback logic and side-effect handling should still be tested like normal application code, but we should change how we assert the generated result.

The Output Contract

When a conventional endpoint calls an external service, we usually rely on a JSON parser and a clearly defined contract. When the service returns invalid JSON or a response that does not match the expected schema, deserialization fails and the application handles the error.

AI output creates a more subtle problem.

A model may return perfectly valid JSON that matches the expected schema but is still logically wrong.

For example:

{
  "approved": true,
  "reason": "The customer meets all required conditions.",
  "failedConditions": [
    {
      "name": "age",
      "value": 13,
      "description": "The customer is under the required age."
    }
  ]
}

This response is valid JSON and may also match the expected schema. But it contradicts itself. The customer is marked as approved even though one of the required conditions has failed.

So schema validation is necessary, but it is not enough.

AI output may require additional levels of validation, such as business-rule validation, logical consistency checks and safety validation.

The endpoint must be able to tell the difference between a response that can be parsed and a response that can actually be trusted.

Observability and Cost

Traditional endpoint monitoring usually focuses on metrics such as request count, latency, errors, dependency calls and resource usage.

AI endpoints need these metrics too, but they also introduce model-specific ones:

  • input and output token usage
  • model and model version
  • number of generation attempts
  • tool calls
  • validation failures
  • repair success rate
  • estimated cost
  • output quality or evaluation score

Without these metrics, it is difficult to understand why an endpoint became slower, more expensive or less reliable.

A provider may change the model behind an alias. Prompts may grow over time, retrieved context may become larger and retry frequency may increase. The endpoint may still return 200 OK while its cost increases and the quality of its output slowly gets worse.

Summary

Traditional endpoints execute business logic that is defined and controlled by code.

AI endpoints are different. They orchestrate probabilistic generation, validate the result and decide whether it is reliable enough to return.

That changes how we should think about and design endpoints that use AI models.

An AI endpoint should not be just a thin proxy around a model call. The model generates the output, but the backend still decides whether it should become the final response.

These are some of the biggest differences I discovered while creating, debugging and maintaining AI endpoints. There are definitely more, but even these few examples show that adding an AI model behind an endpoint can significantly change its flow.

sentences.en: Sending a large context for a specific task costs more and may produce unexpected results.
sentences.zh: 为特定任务发送大型上下文成本更高，并且可能会产生意想不到的结果。

blocks.html_en: So when I started looking closer, especially when I needed reliable structured output and predictable application behavior, I quickly realized that it was not that simple. Validation was no longer only guarding execution, it had also become a post-processing step. AI models are probabilistic. Even with the same input, they may return different outputs, omit required information, misunderstand instructions or return something that is technically valid but logically wrong. And because every token has a price, I cannot simply retry the request and hope for a better result.

sentences.en: So when I started looking closer, especially when I needed reliable structured output and predictable application behavior, I quickly realized that it was not that simple.
sentences.zh: 因此，当我开始仔细观察时，尤其是当我需要可靠的结构化输出和可预测的应用程序行为时，我很快意识到事情并不那么简单。

sentences.en: Validation was no longer only guarding execution, it had also become a post-processing step.
sentences.zh: 验证不再只是保护执行，它也成为一个后处理步骤。

sentences.en: AI models are probabilistic.
sentences.zh: 人工智能模型是概率性的。

sentences.en: Even with the same input, they may return different outputs, omit required information, misunderstand instructions or return something that is technically valid but logically wrong.
sentences.zh: 即使使用相同的输入，它们也可能返回不同的输出、省略所需信息、误解指令或返回技术上有效但逻辑上错误的内容。

sentences.en: And because every token has a price, I cannot simply retry the request and hope for a better result.
sentences.zh: 而且因为每个代币都有价格，所以我不能简单地重试请求并希望得到更好的结果。

blocks.html_en: That was when I started questioning whether AI endpoints should be designed in the same way as conventional Web API endpoints.

sentences.en: That was when I started questioning whether AI endpoints should be designed in the same way as conventional Web API endpoints.
sentences.zh: 从那时起，我开始质疑 AI 端点是否应该以与传统 Web API 端点相同的方式设计。

blocks.html_en: Conventional Web API Endpoint Flow

sentences.en: Conventional Web API Endpoint Flow
sentences.zh: 传统 Web API 端点流程

blocks.html_en: A conventional Web API endpoint usually follows a similar flow:

sentences.en: A conventional Web API endpoint usually follows a similar flow:
sentences.zh: 传统的 Web API 端点通常遵循类似的流程：

sentences.en: Enter fullscreen mode Exit fullscreen mode
sentences.zh: 进入全屏模式 退出全屏模式

blocks.html_en: The first phase is request validation. We validate the incoming data against property constraints, API contracts, authorization rules and application-specific business rules.

sentences.en: The first phase is request validation.
sentences.zh: 第一阶段是请求验证。

sentences.en: We validate the incoming data against property constraints, API contracts, authorization rules and application-specific business rules.
sentences.zh: 我们根据属性约束、API 合约、授权规则和特定于应用程序的业务规则来验证传入数据。

blocks.html_en: The second phase is execution. The application processes data, performs I/O operations or executes business logic.

sentences.en: The second phase is execution.
sentences.zh: 第二阶段是执行。

sentences.en: The application processes data, performs I/O operations or executes business logic.
sentences.zh: 应用程序处理数据、执行 I/O 操作或执行业务逻辑。

blocks.html_en: The final phase is returning a representation of the result. The code executed by a conventional endpoint is normally deterministic within a known application state. When the same code runs against the same state, we generally get the same result.

sentences.en: The final phase is returning a representation of the result.
sentences.zh: 最后阶段是返回结果的表示。

sentences.en: The code executed by a conventional endpoint is normally deterministic within a known application state.
sentences.zh: 传统端点执行的代码通常在已知的应用程序状态内是确定性的。

sentences.en: When the same code runs against the same state, we generally get the same result.
sentences.zh: 当相同的代码针对相同的状态运行时，我们通常会得到相同的结果。

blocks.html_en: And that is basically it.

sentences.en: And that is basically it.
sentences.zh: 基本上就是这样。

blocks.html_en: The general flow of a conventional Web API endpoint is relatively simple, although the individual steps can, of course, be very complex. But AI models do not work in exactly the same way. They do not simply execute a predefined sequence of instructions and always produce the same result.

sentences.en: The general flow of a conventional Web API endpoint is relatively simple, although the individual steps can, of course, be very complex.
sentences.zh: 传统 Web API 端点的一般流程相对简单，但各个步骤当然可能非常复杂。

sentences.en: But AI models do not work in exactly the same way.
sentences.zh: 但人工智能模型的工作方式并不完全相同。

sentences.en: They do not simply execute a predefined sequence of instructions and always produce the same result.
sentences.zh: 它们并不简单地执行预定义的指令序列并且总是产生相同的结果。

blocks.html_en: AI-powered Web API Endpoint Flow

sentences.en: AI-powered Web API Endpoint Flow
sentences.zh: AI 驱动的 Web API 端点流程

blocks.html_en: The internal flow of an AI-powered endpoint is usually more complicated:

sentences.en: The internal flow of an AI-powered endpoint is usually more complicated:
sentences.zh: AI 驱动的端点的内部流程通常更加复杂：

sentences.en: Enter fullscreen mode Exit fullscreen mode
sentences.zh: 进入全屏模式 退出全屏模式

blocks.html_en: We still start by validating the incoming request. Standard property constraints and business rules are still important, but AI endpoints may also require additional controls. The application may need to restrict the requested topic, limit input size, apply controls against suspicious instructions, isolate untrusted retrieved content or decide which tools the model is allowed to call.

sentences.en: We still start by validating the incoming request.
sentences.zh: 我们仍然从验证传入的请求开始。

sentences.en: Standard property constraints and business rules are still important, but AI endpoints may also require additional controls.
sentences.zh: 标准属性约束和业务规则仍然很重要，但人工智能端点可能还需要额外的控制。

sentences.en: The application may need to restrict the requested topic, limit input size, apply controls against suspicious instructions, isolate untrusted retrieved content or decide which tools the model is allowed to call.
sentences.zh: 应用程序可能需要限制请求的主题、限制输入大小、对可疑指令应用控制、隔离不受信任的检索内容或决定允许模型调用哪些工具。

blocks.html_en: The next step is preparing the prompt and settings for the model call. The application prepares the instructions, conversation history, retrieved context, tool definitions and generation settings.

sentences.en: The next step is preparing the prompt and settings for the model call.
sentences.zh: 下一步是准备模型调用的提示和设置。

sentences.en: The application prepares the instructions, conversation history, retrieved context, tool definitions and generation settings.
sentences.zh: 该应用程序准备指令、对话历史记录、检索的上下文、工具定义和生成设置。

blocks.html_en: Then the model produces an output. But unlike the result of conventional business logic, we cannot automatically assume that the output is correct just because the model call succeeded. A successful HTTP response from the model provider only tells us that the model generated something. It does not tell us whether the result is complete, safe, grounded or even useful.

sentences.en: Then the model produces an output.
sentences.zh: 然后模型产生输出。

sentences.en: But unlike the result of conventional business logic, we cannot automatically assume that the output is correct just because the model call succeeded.
sentences.zh: 但与传统业务逻辑的结果不同，我们不能仅仅因为模型调用成功就自动假设输出是正确的。

sentences.en: A successful HTTP response from the model provider only tells us that the model generated something.
sentences.zh: 来自模型提供者的成功 HTTP 响应仅告诉我们模型生成了一些内容。

sentences.en: It does not tell us whether the result is complete, safe, grounded or even useful.
sentences.zh: 它并没有告诉我们结果是否完整、安全、有根据甚至有用。

blocks.html_en: So validation appears again after generation. For example, we may validate the JSON schema in a similar way to a response from a conventional external service. But even when the schema is correct, the result may still be logically wrong for the business domain. The model may also omit properties that are not technically required by the schema but should be present based on the provided context.

sentences.en: So validation appears again after generation.
sentences.zh: 因此验证在生成后再次出现。

sentences.en: For example, we may validate the JSON schema in a similar way to a response from a conventional external service.
sentences.zh: 例如，我们可以以与传统外部服务的响应类似的方式验证 JSON 模式。

sentences.en: But even when the schema is correct, the result may still be logically wrong for the business domain.
sentences.zh: 但即使模式正确，结果对于业务领域来说在逻辑上仍然可能是错误的。

sentences.en: The model may also omit properties that are not technically required by the schema but should be present based on the provided context.
sentences.zh: 该模型还可以省略模式在技术上不需要但应基于所提供的上下文而存在的属性。

blocks.html_en: When the output fails these checks, the application must decide what to do next. It may try to repair the response, ask the model to generate it again, use a fallback model, return a controlled error or send the request for human review. But every decision has its own cost. A retry consumes more tokens, while human intervention costs both time and money.

sentences.en: When the output fails these checks, the application must decide what to do next.
sentences.zh: 当输出未通过这些检查时，应用程序必须决定下一步做什么。

sentences.en: It may try to repair the response, ask the model to generate it again, use a fallback model, return a controlled error or send the request for human review.
sentences.zh: 它可能会尝试修复响应、要求模型再次生成响应、使用后备模型、返回受控错误或发送人工审核请求。

sentences.en: But every decision has its own cost.
sentences.zh: 但每个决定都有其自身的成本。

sentences.en: A retry consumes more tokens, while human intervention costs both time and money.
sentences.zh: 重试会消耗更多的代币，而人为干预会花费时间和金钱。

blocks.html_en: All of this turns the endpoint into an orchestration pipeline instead of a simple proxy around a model call.

sentences.en: All of this turns the endpoint into an orchestration pipeline instead of a simple proxy around a model call.
sentences.zh: 所有这些都将端点变成了编排管道，而不是围绕模型调用的简单代理。

blocks.html_en: What This Difference Changes

sentences.en: What This Difference Changes
sentences.zh: 这种差异会带来什么变化

blocks.html_en: With a conventional Web API, the application explicitly defines how the result is created. This means that the execution path is controlled by code and we know what result to expect.

sentences.en: With a conventional Web API, the application explicitly defines how the result is created.
sentences.zh: 使用传统的 Web API，应用程序显式定义如何创建结果。

sentences.en: This means that the execution path is controlled by code and we know what result to expect.
sentences.zh: 这意味着执行路径是由代码控制的，我们知道期望的结果。

blocks.html_en: With an AI-powered Web API, the application delegates part of the result creation to a probabilistic system. This means that we no longer fully control how the result is generated. To achieve the expected result, we need to add extra steps to the flow, such as post-processing and output validation.

sentences.en: With an AI-powered Web API, the application delegates part of the result creation to a probabilistic system.
sentences.zh: 借助人工智能驱动的 Web API，应用程序将部分结果创建委托给概率系统。

sentences.en: This means that we no longer fully control how the result is generated.
sentences.zh: 这意味着我们不再完全控制结果的生成方式。

sentences.en: To achieve the expected result, we need to add extra steps to the flow, such as post-processing and output validation.
sentences.zh: 为了达到预期的结果，我们需要在流程中添加额外的步骤，例如后处理和输出验证。

blocks.html_en: We can simplify it like this:

sentences.en: We can simplify it like this:
sentences.zh: 我们可以这样简化：

blocks.html_en: • Conventional Web API: the backend executes the rules.

sentences.en: Conventional Web API: the backend executes the rules.
sentences.zh: 传统Web API：后端执行规则。

blocks.html_en: • AI-powered Web API: the backend orchestrates and evaluates the result.

sentences.en: AI-powered Web API: the backend orchestrates and evaluates the result.
sentences.zh: AI 支持的 Web API：后端编排并评估结果。

blocks.html_en: But this difference affects much more than validation. It also changes how we think about latency, retries, idempotency, testing, observability, cost and output contracts.

sentences.en: But this difference affects much more than validation.
sentences.zh: 但这种差异的影响远不止验证。

sentences.en: It also changes how we think about latency, retries, idempotency, testing, observability, cost and output contracts.
sentences.zh: 它还改变了我们对延迟、重试、幂等性、测试、可观察性、成本和输出契约的看法。

blocks.html_en: There are definitely more differences, but let’s have a look at the ones I discovered along the way.

sentences.en: There are definitely more differences, but let’s have a look at the ones I discovered along the way.
sentences.zh: 肯定还有更多差异，但让我们看看我一路上发现的差异。

blocks.html_en: Unpredictable Latency

sentences.en: Unpredictable Latency
sentences.zh: 不可预测的延迟

blocks.html_en: The latency of a conventional endpoint is usually determined by business logic, I/O operations, data processing and similar operations. These operations are not always fast or perfectly predictable, but we can usually measure them separately and optimize the slowest parts by improving the code, optimizing database queries or adding caching.

sentences.en: The latency of a conventional endpoint is usually determined by business logic, I/O operations, data processing and similar operations.
sentences.zh: 传统端点的延迟通常由业务逻辑、I/O操作、数据处理和类似操作决定。

sentences.en: These operations are not always fast or perfectly predictable, but we can usually measure them separately and optimize the slowest parts by improving the code, optimizing database queries or adding caching.
sentences.zh: 这些操作并不总是很快或完全可预测，但我们通常可以单独测量它们，并通过改进代码、优化数据库查询或添加缓存来优化最慢的部分。

blocks.html_en: AI endpoints introduce another level of variability.

sentences.en: AI endpoints introduce another level of variability.
sentences.zh: 人工智能端点引入了另一个层面的可变性。

blocks.html_en: Generation time may depend on the selected model, prompt and context size, output length, provider load and other factors. One request may finish after a single model call, while another may require several tool calls, validation attempts or regeneration steps.

sentences.en: Generation time may depend on the selected model, prompt and context size, output length, provider load and other factors.
sentences.zh: 生成时间可能取决于所选模型、提示和上下文大小、输出长度、提供者负载和其他因素。

sentences.en: One request may finish after a single model call, while another may require several tool calls, validation attempts or regeneration steps.
sentences.zh: 一个请求可能在单个模型调用后完成，而另一个请求可能需要多次工具调用、验证尝试或重新生成步骤。

blocks.html_en: So latency is no longer determined only by the operations we explicitly execute. It may also depend on decisions made during model generation.

sentences.en: So latency is no longer determined only by the operations we explicitly execute.
sentences.zh: 因此，延迟不再仅由我们显式执行的操作决定。

sentences.en: It may also depend on decisions made during model generation.
sentences.zh: 它还可能取决于模型生成期间做出的决策。

blocks.html_en: That makes timeouts, cancellation, streaming, asynchronous processing and latency budgets even more important.

sentences.en: That makes timeouts, cancellation, streaming, asynchronous processing and latency budgets even more important.
sentences.zh: 这使得超时、取消、流式传输、异步处理和延迟预算变得更加重要。

blocks.html_en: One of the easiest ways to improve the latency of an AI-powered endpoint is to improve the prompt. We can make it more specific, reduce unnecessary context and limit the expected output.

sentences.en: One of the easiest ways to improve the latency of an AI-powered endpoint is to improve the prompt.
sentences.zh: 改善人工智能端点延迟的最简单方法之一是改善提示。

sentences.en: We can make it more specific, reduce unnecessary context and limit the expected output.
sentences.zh: 我们可以使其更加具体，减少不必要的上下文并限制预期的输出。

blocks.html_en: Another example is avoiding the need for the model to return full objects.

sentences.en: Another example is avoiding the need for the model to return full objects.
sentences.zh: 另一个例子是避免模型返回完整对象的需要。

blocks.html_en: For example, we may provide data like this:

sentences.en: For example, we may provide data like this:
sentences.zh: 例如，我们可能提供如下数据：

sentences.en: Enter fullscreen mode Exit fullscreen mode
sentences.zh: 进入全屏模式 退出全屏模式

blocks.html_en: Then we can instruct the model to return only the selected IDs instead of repeating the full objects.

sentences.en: Then we can instruct the model to return only the selected IDs instead of repeating the full objects.
sentences.zh: 然后我们可以指示模型仅返回选定的 ID，而不是重复完整的对象。

blocks.html_en: The backend can map those IDs back to the original data after generation. This reduces the number of output tokens and may improve both latency and cost.

sentences.en: The backend can map those IDs back to the original data after generation.
sentences.zh: 后端可以在生成后将这些 ID 映射回原始数据。

sentences.en: This reduces the number of output tokens and may improve both latency and cost.
sentences.zh: 这减少了输出令牌的数量，并且可以改善延迟和成本。

blocks.html_en: The main point is that we approach latency optimization differently. With conventional endpoints, we usually optimize code, database access or caching. With AI endpoints, we also need to optimize prompts, context size, model selection and generated output.

sentences.en: The main point is that we approach latency optimization differently.
sentences.zh: 要点是我们以不同的方式处理延迟优化。

sentences.en: With conventional endpoints, we usually optimize code, database access or caching.
sentences.zh: 对于传统端点，我们通常会优化代码、数据库访问或缓存。

sentences.en: With AI endpoints, we also need to optimize prompts, context size, model selection and generated output.
sentences.zh: 对于人工智能端点，我们还需要优化提示、上下文大小、模型选择和生成的输出。

blocks.html_en: Retry Logic

sentences.en: Retry Logic
sentences.zh: 重试逻辑

blocks.html_en: In conventional Web APIs, we often retry I/O operations under specific conditions. For example, we may retry when a dependency is temporarily unavailable, a connection is interrupted or an external service returns a transient status code. These retries are usually based on technical failures.

sentences.en: In conventional Web APIs, we often retry I/O operations under specific conditions.
sentences.zh: 在传统的Web API中，我们经常在特定条件下重试I/O操作。

sentences.en: For example, we may retry when a dependency is temporarily unavailable, a connection is interrupted or an external service returns a transient status code.
sentences.zh: 例如，当依赖项暂时不可用、连接中断或外部服务返回暂时状态代码时，我们可能会重试。

sentences.en: These retries are usually based on technical failures.
sentences.zh: 这些重试通常是基于技术故障。

blocks.html_en: AI endpoints introduce another type of retry. The request may complete successfully at the transport level, but the generated output may still be unusable. For example, it may miss a required field, break the expected schema, contradict the supplied context or fail a business rule.

sentences.en: AI endpoints introduce another type of retry.
sentences.zh: AI端点引入了另一种类型的重试。

sentences.en: The request may complete successfully at the transport level, but the generated output may still be unusable.
sentences.zh: 请求可能在传输级别成功完成，但生成的输出可能仍然不可用。

sentences.en: For example, it may miss a required field, break the expected schema, contradict the supplied context or fail a business rule.
sentences.zh: 例如，它可能会错过必填字段、破坏预期模式、与提供的上下文相矛盾或使业务规则失败。

blocks.html_en: Every retry increases latency and consumes additional tokens, which means additional cost. The next attempt may return a different but still incorrect response.

sentences.en: Every retry increases latency and consumes additional tokens, which means additional cost.
sentences.zh: 每次重试都会增加延迟并消耗额外的令牌，这意味着额外的成本。

sentences.en: The next attempt may return a different but still incorrect response.
sentences.zh: 下一次尝试可能会返回不同但仍然不正确的响应。

blocks.html_en: Because of that, AI retries should not be treated like ordinary network retries. They need explicit limits, and we should also consider additional constraints such as the token and monetary budget, the reason for the failure, whether another attempt is likely to help, and whether a fallback model or deterministic alternative is available.

sentences.en: Because of that, AI retries should not be treated like ordinary network retries.
sentences.zh: 因此，人工智能重试不应被视为普通网络重试。

sentences.en: They need explicit limits, and we should also consider additional constraints such as the token and monetary budget, the reason for the failure, whether another attempt is likely to help, and whether a fallback model or deterministic alternative is available.
sentences.zh: 它们需要明确的限制，我们还应该考虑额外的约束，例如代币和货币预算、失败的原因、另一次尝试是否可能有所帮助，以及是否有后备模型或确定性替代方案可用。

blocks.html_en: Let’s have a look at this C# retry pipeline, which I used with a local model exposed through Ollama:

sentences.en: Let’s have a look at this C# retry pipeline, which I used with a local model exposed through Ollama:
sentences.zh: 让我们看一下这个 C# 重试管道，我将其与通过 Ollama 公开的本地模型一起使用：

sentences.en: Enter fullscreen mode Exit fullscreen mode
sentences.zh: 进入全屏模式 退出全屏模式

blocks.html_en: The pipeline retries when it receives an HttpRequestException, JsonException or TaskCanceledException. It allows a maximum of two retry attempts and uses exponential backoff with an initial delay of two seconds.

sentences.en: The pipeline retries when it receives an HttpRequestException, JsonException or TaskCanceledException.
sentences.zh: 当管道收到 HttpRequestException、JsonException 或 TaskCanceledException 时，它将重试。

sentences.en: It allows a maximum of two retry attempts and uses exponential backoff with an initial delay of two seconds.
sentences.zh: 它允许最多两次重试，并使用指数退避，初始延迟为两秒。

blocks.html_en: So far, this looks like a typical resilience pipeline for a conventional API endpoint.

sentences.en: So far, this looks like a typical resilience pipeline for a conventional API endpoint.
sentences.zh: 到目前为止，这看起来像是传统 API 端点的典型弹性管道。

blocks.html_en: The important difference is this condition:

sentences.en: The important difference is this condition:
sentences.zh: 重要的区别是这个条件：

blocks.html_en: .HandleResult(r => r.IsFailed)

sentences.en: .HandleResult(r => r.IsFailed)

blocks.html_en: It also retries when the operation does not throw an exception but still returns a failed result.

sentences.en: It also retries when the operation does not throw an exception but still returns a failed result.
sentences.zh: 当操作未引发异常但仍返回失败结果时，它也会重试。

blocks.html_en: Inside the action executed by this pipeline, I validate the AI output. If the response is technically valid but logically wrong, I return a failed result and let the pipeline try again.

sentences.en: Inside the action executed by this pipeline, I validate the AI output.
sentences.zh: 在该管道执行的操作中，我验证 AI 输出。

sentences.en: If the response is technically valid but logically wrong, I return a failed result and let the pipeline try again.
sentences.zh: 如果响应在技术上有效但在逻辑上错误，我将返回失败的结果并让管道重试。

blocks.html_en: This approach may not be ideal when using a paid model provider because every retry consumes additional tokens and increases the cost. But for a local or free model, a small and strictly limited number of retries may be sufficient.

sentences.en: This approach may not be ideal when using a paid model provider because every retry consumes additional tokens and increases the cost.
sentences.zh: 当使用付费模型提供商时，这种方法可能并不理想，因为每次重试都会消耗额外的令牌并增加成本。

sentences.en: But for a local or free model, a small and strictly limited number of retries may be sufficient.
sentences.zh: 但对于本地或免费模型，少量且严格限制的重试次数可能就足够了。

blocks.html_en: Sometimes the correct decision is not to retry. It may be better to reject the output, return a controlled error or ask the user for more information.

sentences.en: Sometimes the correct decision is not to retry.
sentences.zh: 有时正确的决定不是重试。

sentences.en: It may be better to reject the output, return a controlled error or ask the user for more information.
sentences.zh: 最好拒绝输出、返回受控错误或询问用户更多信息。

blocks.html_en: Idempotency and Side Effects

sentences.en: Idempotency and Side Effects
sentences.zh: 幂等性和副作用

blocks.html_en: Idempotency means that executing the same operation multiple times has the same effect as executing it once.

sentences.en: Idempotency means that executing the same operation multiple times has the same effect as executing it once.
sentences.zh: 幂等性是指多次执行同一个操作与执行一次效果相同。

blocks.html_en: A simple example is a conventional GET endpoint:

sentences.en: A simple example is a conventional GET endpoint:
sentences.zh: 一个简单的例子是传统的 GET 端点：

sentences.zh: 进入全屏模式 退出全屏模式

blocks.html_en: We can call this endpoint several times without changing the order. The returned representation may change if the underlying data changes, but the request itself does not produce additional side effects.

sentences.en: We can call this endpoint several times without changing the order.
sentences.zh: 我们可以多次调用该端点，而无需更改顺序。

sentences.en: The returned representation may change if the underlying data changes, but the request itself does not produce additional side effects.
sentences.zh: 如果底层数据发生变化，返回的表示可能会发生变化，但请求本身不会产生额外的副作用。

blocks.html_en: With AI endpoints, idempotency becomes more complicated when the model can call tools.

sentences.en: With AI endpoints, idempotency becomes more complicated when the model can call tools.
sentences.zh: 对于人工智能端点，当模型可以调用工具时，幂等性变得更加复杂。

blocks.html_en: Sending the same prompt multiple times may produce different outputs or slightly different wording. For read-only endpoints, this may be acceptable because no server state is changed.

sentences.en: Sending the same prompt multiple times may produce different outputs or slightly different wording.
sentences.zh: 多次发送相同的提示可能会产生不同的输出或略有不同的措辞。

sentences.en: For read-only endpoints, this may be acceptable because no server state is changed.
sentences.zh: 对于只读端点，这可能是可以接受的，因为服务器状态没有改变。

blocks.html_en: But it becomes much more dangerous when the endpoint can perform actions. For example, the model may create an order, send an email or update data through a tool. If output validation later fails and the whole operation is retried, the model may call the same tool again and create a second order.

sentences.en: But it becomes much more dangerous when the endpoint can perform actions.
sentences.zh: 但当端点可以执行操作时，它就变得更加危险。

sentences.en: For example, the model may create an order, send an email or update data through a tool.
sentences.zh: 例如，模型可以创建订单、发送电子邮件或通过工具更新数据。

sentences.en: If output validation later fails and the whole operation is retried, the model may call the same tool again and create a second order.
sentences.zh: 如果稍后输出验证失败并且重试整个操作，则模型可能会再次调用相同的工具并创建第二个订单。

blocks.html_en: That is why actions triggered by AI endpoints should use protections such as idempotency keys, persisted operation results and unique constraints. Repeating the same action with the same operation ID should return the original result instead of executing the side effect again.

sentences.en: That is why actions triggered by AI endpoints should use protections such as idempotency keys, persisted operation results and unique constraints.
sentences.zh: 这就是为什么人工智能端点触发的操作应该使用幂等性密钥、持久操作结果和唯一约束等保护措施。

sentences.en: Repeating the same action with the same operation ID should return the original result instead of executing the side effect again.
sentences.zh: 使用相同的操作 ID 重复相同的操作应该返回原始结果，而不是再次执行副作用。

blocks.html_en: Testing AI Endpoints

sentences.en: Testing AI Endpoints
sentences.zh: 测试 AI 端点

blocks.html_en: Testing conventional endpoints is relatively straightforward when the application state and dependencies are controlled.

sentences.en: Testing conventional endpoints is relatively straightforward when the application state and dependencies are controlled.
sentences.zh: 当应用程序状态和依赖性受到控制时，测试传统端点相对简单。

blocks.html_en: We prepare the data, execute the endpoint and assert the expected result, such as the status code, expected response values, an updated application state or whether an external dependency was called.

sentences.en: We prepare the data, execute the endpoint and assert the expected result, such as the status code, expected response values, an updated application state or whether an external dependency was called.
sentences.zh: 我们准备数据、执行端点并断言预期结果，例如状态代码、预期响应值、更新的应用程序状态或是否调用外部依赖项。

blocks.html_en: With AI endpoints, exact-output assertions are often fragile. The same valid answer may be written in many different ways. A model update may also change the wording while keeping the same meaning. But asserting only the response status or checking that fields are not null is too weak.

sentences.en: With AI endpoints, exact-output assertions are often fragile.
sentences.zh: 对于人工智能端点，精确的输出断言通常很脆弱。

sentences.en: The same valid answer may be written in many different ways.
sentences.zh: 同一个有效答案可以用多种不同的方式来写。

sentences.en: A model update may also change the wording while keeping the same meaning.
sentences.zh: 模型更新也可能会更改措辞，同时保持相同的含义。

sentences.en: But asserting only the response status or checking that fields are not null is too weak.
sentences.zh: 但是仅断言响应状态或检查字段不为空太弱了。

blocks.html_en: Instead of always comparing the exact response, we can verify the required properties of the result. Depending on the use case, we may assert that values are within an allowed range, forbidden content is not present, tool calls respect the allowlist and the response follows the expected business rules.

sentences.en: Instead of always comparing the exact response, we can verify the required properties of the result.
sentences.zh: 我们可以验证结果所需的属性，而不是总是比较确切的响应。

sentences.zh: 人工智能输出可能需要额外级别的验证，例如业务规则验证、逻辑一致性检查和安全验证。

blocks.html_en: Observability and Cost

sentences.en: Observability and Cost
sentences.zh: 可观察性和成本

blocks.html_en: Traditional endpoint monitoring usually focuses on metrics such as request count, latency, errors, dependency calls and resource usage.

sentences.en: Traditional endpoint monitoring usually focuses on metrics such as request count, latency, errors, dependency calls and resource usage.
sentences.zh: 传统的端点监控通常侧重于请求计数、延迟、错误、依赖项调用和资源使用等指标。

sentences.en: AI endpoints need these metrics too, but they also introduce model-specific ones:

sentences.zh: 输出质量或评价分数

blocks.html_en: Without these metrics, it is difficult to understand why an endpoint became slower, more expensive or less reliable.

sentences.zh: 如果没有这些指标，就很难理解为什么端点变得更慢、更昂贵或更不可靠。

blocks.html_en: A provider may change the model behind an alias. Prompts may grow over time, retrieved context may become larger and retry frequency may increase. The endpoint may still return 200 OK while its cost increases and the quality of its output slowly gets worse.

sentences.en: A provider may change the model behind an alias.
sentences.zh: 提供者可以更改别名背后的模型。

blocks.html_en: Summary

sentences.en: Summary
sentences.zh: 概括

blocks.html_en: Traditional endpoints execute business logic that is defined and controlled by code.

sentences.en: Traditional endpoints execute business logic that is defined and controlled by code.
sentences.zh: 传统端点执行由代码定义和控制的业务逻辑。

blocks.html_en: AI endpoints are different. They orchestrate probabilistic generation, validate the result and decide whether it is reliable enough to return.

sentences.en: AI endpoints are different.
sentences.zh: AI端点不同。

sentences.en: They orchestrate probabilistic generation, validate the result and decide whether it is reliable enough to return.
sentences.zh: 他们协调概率生成，验证结果并决定其是否足够可靠以返回。

blocks.html_en: That changes how we should think about and design endpoints that use AI models.

sentences.en: That changes how we should think about and design endpoints that use AI models.
sentences.zh: 这改变了我们思考和设计使用人工智能模型的端点的方式。

blocks.html_en: An AI endpoint should not be just a thin proxy around a model call. The model generates the output, but the backend still decides whether it should become the final response.

sentences.en: An AI endpoint should not be just a thin proxy around a model call.
sentences.zh: AI 端点不应该只是模型调用周围的瘦代理。

sentences.en: The model generates the output, but the backend still decides whether it should become the final response.
sentences.zh: 模型生成输出，但后端仍然决定它是否应该成为最终响应。

blocks.html_en: These are some of the biggest differences I discovered while creating, debugging and maintaining AI endpoints. There are definitely more, but even these few examples show that adding an AI model behind an endpoint can significantly change its flow.

sentences.en: These are some of the biggest differences I discovered while creating, debugging and maintaining AI endpoints.
sentences.zh: 这些是我在创建、调试和维护人工智能端点时发现的一些最大差异。

sentences.en: There are definitely more, but even these few examples show that adding an AI model behind an endpoint can significantly change its flow.
sentences.zh: 肯定还有更多，但即使这几个例子也表明，在端点后面添加人工智能模型可以显着改变其流程。

content_html_zh:
作为一名后端开发人员，我已经构建了数百个端点，因此典型的端点流程在我对 Web 应用程序的看法中根深蒂固。 但当我开始构建人工智能驱动的端点时，我注意到一个有趣的转变。

起初，AI 端点看起来像简单的代理端点，具有一些用于连接到模型的配置：

API receives request
↓
send prompt to model
↓
receive response
↓
return it to the client

进入全屏模式 退出全屏模式

它运行得很好，直到我发现直接从客户那里传递提示并不是一个好主意。 该端点可能会被滥用于完全不同的目的，从而允许其他人消耗我的人工智能使用积分。

然后我意识到输入也需要限制。 为特定任务发送大型上下文成本更高，并且可能会产生意想不到的结果。

因此，当我开始仔细观察时，尤其是当我需要可靠的结构化输出和可预测的应用程序行为时，我很快意识到事情并不那么简单。 验证不再只是保护执行，它也成为一个后处理步骤。 人工智能模型是概率性的。 即使使用相同的输入，它们也可能返回不同的输出、省略所需信息、误解指令或返回技术上有效但逻辑上错误的内容。 而且因为每个代币都有价格，所以我不能简单地重试请求并希望得到更好的结果。

从那时起，我开始质疑 AI 端点是否应该以与传统 Web API 端点相同的方式设计。

  • 这种差异会改变什么 不可预测的延迟 重试逻辑 幂等性和副作用 测试 AI 端点 输出合约可观察性和成本
  • 概括

传统 Web API 端点流程

传统的 Web API 端点通常遵循类似的流程：

validate request
↓
execute business logic
↓
return representation

进入全屏模式 退出全屏模式

第一阶段是请求验证。 我们根据属性约束、API 合约、授权规则和特定于应用程序的业务规则来验证传入数据。

第二阶段是执行。 应用程序处理数据、执行 I/O 操作或执行业务逻辑。

最后阶段是返回结果的表示。 传统端点执行的代码通常在已知的应用程序状态内是确定性的。 当相同的代码针对相同的状态运行时，我们通常会得到相同的结果。

基本上就是这样。

传统 Web API 端点的一般流程相对简单，但各个步骤当然可能非常复杂。 但人工智能模型的工作方式并不完全相同。 它们并不简单地执行预定义的指令序列并且总是产生相同的结果。

AI 驱动的 Web API 端点流程

AI 驱动的端点的内部流程通常更加复杂：

validate request
↓
prepare prompt, tools and context
↓
generate probabilistic output
↓
validate schema, meaning and safety
↓
retry, repair, reject or fall back
↓
return representation

进入全屏模式 退出全屏模式

我们仍然从验证传入的请求开始。 标准属性约束和业务规则仍然很重要，但人工智能端点可能还需要额外的控制。 应用程序可能需要限制请求的主题、限制输入大小、对可疑指令应用控制、隔离不受信任的检索内容或决定允许模型调用哪些工具。

下一步是准备模型调用的提示和设置。 该应用程序准备指令、对话历史记录、检索的上下文、工具定义和生成设置。

然后模型产生输出。 但与传统业务逻辑的结果不同，我们不能仅仅因为模型调用成功就自动假设输出是正确的。 来自模型提供者的成功 HTTP 响应仅告诉我们模型生成了一些内容。 它并没有告诉我们结果是否完整、安全、有根据甚至有用。

因此验证在生成后再次出现。 例如，我们可以以与传统外部服务的响应类似的方式验证 JSON 模式。 但即使模式正确，结果对于业务领域来说在逻辑上仍然可能是错误的。 该模型还可以省略模式在技术上不需要但应基于所提供的上下文而存在的属性。

当输出未通过这些检查时，应用程序必须决定下一步做什么。 它可能会尝试修复响应、要求模型再次生成响应、使用后备模型、返回受控错误或发送人工审核请求。 但每个决定都有其自身的成本。 重试会消耗更多的代币，而人为干预会花费时间和金钱。

所有这些都将端点变成了编排管道，而不是围绕模型调用的简单代理。

这种差异会带来什么变化

使用传统的 Web API，应用程序显式定义如何创建结果。 这意味着执行路径是由代码控制的，我们知道期望的结果。

借助人工智能驱动的 Web API，应用程序将部分结果创建委托给概率系统。 这意味着我们不再完全控制结果的生成方式。 为了达到预期的结果，我们需要在流程中添加额外的步骤，例如后处理和输出验证。

我们可以这样简化：

  • 传统Web API：后端执行规则。
  • AI 支持的 Web API：后端编排并评估结果。

但这种差异的影响远不止验证。 它还改变了我们对延迟、重试、幂等性、测试、可观察性、成本和输出契约的看法。

肯定还有更多差异，但让我们看看我一路上发现的差异。

不可预测的延迟

传统端点的延迟通常由业务逻辑、I/O操作、数据处理和类似操作决定。 这些操作并不总是很快或完全可预测，但我们通常可以单独测量它们，并通过改进代码、优化数据库查询或添加缓存来优化最慢的部分。

人工智能端点引入了另一个层面的可变性。

生成时间可能取决于所选模型、提示和上下文大小、输出长度、提供者负载和其他因素。 一个请求可能在单个模型调用后完成，而另一个请求可能需要多次工具调用、验证尝试或重新生成步骤。

因此，延迟不再仅由我们显式执行的操作决定。 它还可能取决于模型生成期间做出的决策。

这使得超时、取消、流式传输、异步处理和延迟预算变得更加重要。

改善人工智能端点延迟的最简单方法之一是改善提示。 我们可以使其更加具体，减少不必要的上下文并限制预期的输出。

另一个例子是避免模型返回完整对象的需要。

例如，我们可能提供如下数据：

{
  "data": [
    {
      "id": 1,
      "name": "john doe",
      "address": "Mordor",
      "category": "Nazgûl"
    },
    {
      "id": 2,
      "name": "joe doe",
      "address": "Gondor",
      "category": "soldier"
    }
  ]
}

然后我们可以指示模型仅返回选定的 ID，而不是重复完整的对象。

后端可以在生成后将这些 ID 映射回原始数据。 这减少了输出令牌的数量，并且可以改善延迟和成本。

要点是我们以不同的方式处理延迟优化。 对于传统端点，我们通常会优化代码、数据库访问或缓存。 对于人工智能端点，我们还需要优化提示、上下文大小、模型选择和生成的输出。

重试逻辑

在传统的Web API中，我们经常在特定条件下重试I/O操作。 例如，当依赖项暂时不可用、连接中断或外部服务返回暂时状态代码时，我们可能会重试。 这些重试通常是基于技术故障。

AI端点引入了另一种类型的重试。 请求可能在传输级别成功完成，但生成的输出可能仍然不可用。 例如，它可能会错过必填字段、破坏预期模式、与提供的上下文相矛盾或使业务规则失败。

每次重试都会增加延迟并消耗额外的令牌，这意味着额外的成本。 下一次尝试可能会返回不同但仍然不正确的响应。

因此，人工智能重试不应被视为普通网络重试。 它们需要明确的限制，我们还应该考虑额外的约束，例如代币和货币预算、失败的原因、另一次尝试是否可能有所帮助，以及是否有后备模型或确定性替代方案可用。

让我们看一下这个 C# 重试管道，我将其与通过 Ollama 公开的本地模型一起使用：

private static class RetryPipeline<T>
{
    public static readonly ResiliencePipeline<Result<T>> Instance = new ResiliencePipelineBuilder<Result<T>>()
        .AddRetry(new RetryStrategyOptions<Result<T>>
        {
            MaxRetryAttempts = 2,
            Delay = TimeSpan.FromSeconds(2),
            BackoffType = DelayBackoffType.Exponential,
            UseJitter = true,
            ShouldHandle = new PredicateBuilder<Result<T>>()
                .Handle<HttpRequestException>()
                .Handle<JsonException>()
                .Handle<TaskCanceledException>(ex =>
                   !ex.CancellationToken.IsCancellationRequested)
                .HandleResult(r => r.IsFailed)
        })
        .Build();
}

进入全屏模式 退出全屏模式

当管道收到 HttpRequestException、JsonException 或 TaskCanceledException 时，它将重试。 它允许最多两次重试，并使用指数退避，初始延迟为两秒。

到目前为止，这看起来像是传统 API 端点的典型弹性管道。

重要的区别是这个条件：

.HandleResult(r => r.IsFailed)

当操作未引发异常但仍返回失败结果时，它也会重试。

在该管道执行的操作中，我验证 AI 输出。 如果响应在技术上有效但在逻辑上错误，我将返回失败的结果并让管道重试。

当使用付费模型提供商时，这种方法可能并不理想，因为每次重试都会消耗额外的令牌并增加成本。 但对于本地或免费模型，少量且严格限制的重试次数可能就足够了。

有时正确的决定不是重试。 最好拒绝输出、返回受控错误或询问用户更多信息。

幂等性和副作用

幂等性是指多次执行同一个操作与执行一次效果相同。

一个简单的例子是传统的 GET 端点：

GET /api/orders/123

进入全屏模式 退出全屏模式

我们可以多次调用该端点，而无需更改顺序。 如果底层数据发生变化，返回的表示可能会发生变化，但请求本身不会产生额外的副作用。

对于人工智能端点，当模型可以调用工具时，幂等性变得更加复杂。

多次发送相同的提示可能会产生不同的输出或略有不同的措辞。 对于只读端点，这可能是可以接受的，因为服务器状态没有改变。

但当端点可以执行操作时，它就变得更加危险。 例如，模型可以创建订单、发送电子邮件或通过工具更新数据。 如果稍后输出验证失败并且重试整个操作，则模型可能会再次调用相同的工具并创建第二个订单。

这就是为什么人工智能端点触发的操作应该使用幂等性密钥、持久操作结果和唯一约束等保护措施。 使用相同的操作 ID 重复相同的操作应该返回原始结果，而不是再次执行副作用。

测试 AI 端点

当应用程序状态和依赖性受到控制时，测试传统端点相对简单。

我们准备数据、执行端点并断言预期结果，例如状态代码、预期响应值、更新的应用程序状态或是否调用外部依赖项。

对于人工智能端点，精确的输出断言通常很脆弱。 同一个有效答案可以用多种不同的方式来写。 模型更新也可能会更改措辞，同时保持相同的含义。 但是仅断言响应状态或检查字段不为空太弱了。

我们可以验证结果所需的属性，而不是总是比较确切的响应。 根据用例，我们可以断言值在允许的范围内，不存在禁止的内容，工具调用遵循允许列表，并且响应遵循预期的业务规则。

例如，使用 Assert.Equal 断言完整响应将是脆弱的：

var result = await endpoint.GeneratePlanAsync(request);

// Fragile assertion
Assert.Equal("Family Day in Brno", result.Title);
Assert.Equal(
    "Visit the science centre and have lunch nearby.",
    result.Description);
Assert.Equal(1_500, result.TotalCost);

进入全屏模式 退出全屏模式

该模型可能会返回不同的标题或描述，同时仍然生成完全有效的计划。

我们可以使用 InRange、Contains 和DoesNotContain 等方法断言对应用程序重要的属性，而不是检查确切的响应：

var result = await endpoint.GeneratePlanAsync(request);

Assert.NotNull(result);
Assert.NotEmpty(result.Activities);

//Assert
Assert.InRange(result.TotalCost,0,request.Budget);

Assert.All(
    result.Activities,
    activity =>
    {
        Assert.Contains(
            activity.Type,
            request.AllowedActivityTypes);

        Assert.InRange(
            activity.TravelTimeMinutes,
            0,
            request.MaximumTravelMinutes);

        Assert.False(
            string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(activity.Name));
    });

Assert.DoesNotContain(result.Activities, activity => activity.IsClosed);
Assert.All(result.Activities,activity => Assert.NotEmpty(activity.SourceIds));

进入全屏模式 退出全屏模式

活动的标题、描述或顺序可能会发生变化，但生成的计划仍然必须遵守预算、旅行时间限制、允许的活动类型和提供的源数据。

端点的很大一部分仍然可以进行确定性测试。 模式验证、授权、工具权限、解析、回退逻辑和副作用处理仍然应该像普通应用程序代码一样进行测试，但我们应该改变断言生成结果的方式。

产出合约

当传统端点调用外部服务时，我们通常依赖于 JSON 解析器和明确定义的契约。 当服务返回无效的 JSON 或与预期架构不匹配的响应时，反序列化会失败并且应用程序会处理错误。

人工智能输出产生了一个更微妙的问题。

模型可能会返回与预期模式匹配的完全有效的 JSON，但在逻辑上仍然是错误的。

例如：

{
  "approved": true,
  "reason": "The customer meets all required conditions.",
  "failedConditions": [
    {
      "name": "age",
      "value": 13,
      "description": "The customer is under the required age."
    }
  ]
}

进入全屏模式 退出全屏模式

此响应是有效的 JSON，也可能与预期的架构匹配。 但这是自相矛盾的。 即使所需条件之一未满足，客户也会被标记为已批准。

因此模式验证是必要的，但这还不够。

人工智能输出可能需要额外级别的验证，例如业务规则验证、逻辑一致性检查和安全验证。

端点必须能够区分可解析的响应和实际可信的响应。

可观察性和成本

传统的端点监控通常侧重于请求计数、延迟、错误、依赖项调用和资源使用等指标。

AI 端点也需要这些指标，但它们还引入了特定于模型的指标：

  • 输入和输出令牌的使用
  • 型号和型号版本
  • 生成尝试次数
  • 工具调用
  • 验证失败
  • 修复成功率
  • 预计费用
  • 输出质量或评价分数

如果没有这些指标，就很难理解为什么端点变得更慢、更昂贵或更不可靠。

提供者可以更改别名背后的模型。 提示可能会随着时间的推移而增加，检索到的上下文可能会变得更大，并且重试频率可能会增加。 端点可能仍会返回 200 OK，但其成本会增加且输出质量会慢慢变差。

概括

传统端点执行由代码定义和控制的业务逻辑。

AI端点不同。 他们协调概率生成，验证结果并决定其是否足够可靠以返回。

这改变了我们思考和设计使用人工智能模型的端点的方式。

AI 端点不应该只是模型调用周围的瘦代理。 模型生成输出，但后端仍然决定它是否应该成为最终响应。

这些是我在创建、调试和维护人工智能端点时发现的一些最大差异。 肯定还有更多，但即使这几个例子也表明，在端点后面添加人工智能模型可以显着改变其流程。
